notes: Human-ratified labels (2026-07-08). Ground truth established by source-inspection of the reference SQLite server (modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived, src/sqlite/src/mcp_server_sqlite/server.py) and a live stdio tools/list capture (server sqlite 0.1.0, protocol 2025-06-18) on 2026-07-08. The server advertises NO MCP annotations on any tool. read_query runs SELECT, write_query runs INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, create_table runs CREATE TABLE, list_tables/describe_table read the schema, and append_insight appends to an in-memory memo. This row is a true-positive recall check: write_query is derived mutating from its name and declares no destructiveHint, so the absent hint is an honest gap a destructive-absent nudge should surface. Its siblings are honestly clean (reads and additive creates carry no destructive claim to contradict), so the row also guards precision — a correct linter flags exactly one of the six tools.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived, src/sqlite/src/mcp_server_sqlite/server.py — read_query executes a SELECT and rejects non-SELECT statements at runtime; no annotations are declared
provenance.notes: Read-only over local SQLite state; nothing to declare, so the silence is honest.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived, src/sqlite/src/mcp_server_sqlite/server.py — write_query executes an arbitrary INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE (it rejects SELECT), so it can remove rows; no readOnlyHint or destructiveHint is declared
provenance.notes: The name write derives mutating; the query body may DELETE rows, so the true destructiveness is at least deleting. Either way no destructiveHint is declared, so the honest verdict is undeclared and a destructive-absent nudge is correct. This is the write-family recall case: without the write lexeme the tool would derive unknown and no nudge would fire.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived, src/sqlite/src/mcp_server_sqlite/server.py — create_table executes a CREATE TABLE statement, adding a new table without changing existing rows; no annotations are declared
provenance.notes: Additive creation carries no destructive claim to be honest or dishonest about, so the absent destructiveHint is not a finding.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived, src/sqlite/src/mcp_server_sqlite/server.py — list_tables reads sqlite_master to enumerate tables; no annotations are declared
provenance.notes: Read-only over local schema; nothing to declare.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived, src/sqlite/src/mcp_server_sqlite/server.py — describe_table runs PRAGMA table_info to read a table's schema; no annotations are declared
provenance.notes: Read-only over local schema; nothing to declare.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived, src/sqlite/src/mcp_server_sqlite/server.py — append_insight appends a string to an in-memory insights list surfaced through a memo resource; it does not write the database and declares no annotations
provenance.notes: Additive append to in-server memo state; no destructive claim to contradict, so the silence is honest.
